title: Could I But Ride Indefinite,
content:
Could I but ride indefinite,
    As doth the meadow-bee,
    And visit only where I liked,
    And no man visit me,

    And flirt all day with buttercups,
    And marry whom I may,
    And dwell a little everywhere,
    Or better, run away

    With no police to follow,
    Or chase me if I do,
    Till I should jump peninsulas
    To get away from you, --

    I said, but just to be a bee
    Upon a raft of air,
    And row in nowhere all day long,
    And anchor off the bar,--
    What liberty! So captives deem
    Who tight in dungeons are.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Emily Elizabeth Dickinson delivers a powerful performance in "Could I But Ride Indefinite,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
